royalty_basis: Greater of $600/month or 6% of Net Sales
actual Gross Revenues of the previous month, subject to annual reconciliation based on Minimum Annual Gross Revenues
10% of Gross Revenues or $1,950 base minimum/month
7% declining to 3% of Gross Sales based on annual volume; subject to yearly minimum
Greater of 6% of Gross Sales/month or minimum royalty (starting at $500/month in year 1)
5% of Gross Revenue if below $48,000/month; 4.5% between $48,000-$95,999; 4% above $96,000; or Minimum Royalty ($50/week in year 1, $100/week year 2, $200/week thereafter), whichever is greater
Declining scale from 6% on first $1.65M Gross Revenue down to 2% above $100M; minimum $15,000/year after year 1
2% on National/Key Account and Farm Class Tire sales; 2% on Excess Service Dept Sales; 3.5%-5.0% on Adjusted Gross Sales (Royalty Matrix based on sales volume)

Greater of 3.5% of monthly Gross Revenue or flat minimum: Tier 1 $2,500/mo, Tier 2 $1,875/mo, Tier 3 $1,250/mo. Flat minimum applies until Dec 31, 2025; 3.5% + minimum kicks in from Jan 1, 2026. Minimum may increase by CPI on April 1 annually.
Gross Sales. Veterans Program: 2.5% first full year of first restaurant, then standard 4.5% thereafter.

Gross Sales. Standard offering: 10% months 1–24, 12% months 25+. Reduced offering: 15% of Gross Sales (all months). Both tiers subject to escalating monthly minimums: Standard $300/mo (months 3–12) rising to $1,500/mo (months 49+); Reduced $475/mo (months 3–12) rising to $2,350/mo (months 49+). Paid monthly, 2 months in arrears.
Gross Revenue (per transaction close); Property Management Services assessed separately at 1.5% of Gross Revenue from Property Management
Gross Sales, 4%-7% sliding scale. Minimum monthly royalty applies from month 13. Rate varies by performance tier.

Gross Receipts — formula-based: Base Operating Service Fee = 15% of Gross Receipts minus equipment rental and business services fee; Additional Operating Service Fee = 50% of Net Profit. Chick-fil-A allocates a portion of the Base Operating Service Fee as a royalty for intellectual property sublicense.

Gross Revenue — 6% on first $1M GR/year per office; 3% on GR exceeding $1M/year. Minimum quarterly: shortfall paid if calculated royalty < $7,500/quarter (equivalent to $125K GR/quarter). Commercial Property Management Services: 1.5% of Gross Revenue.
weekly Royalty Sales (total receipts from all sales of pizza, beverages and other products/services, excluding sales taxes, approved coupons/discounts)

Express retains 40% of Gross Margin (temp/contract staffing) and 10% of Gross Receipts (direct hire); franchisee keeps 60% of Gross Margin + 90% of Gross Receipts. Opt-out model available: 8% of Total Billings royalty. Express handles all billing, payroll, workers' comp, and insurance.

Gross Revenues; from month 4 onward: greater of 5% actual GR or 5% × Minimum Performance Standard. Minimum Performance Standards (monthly GR): months 0-3=$0, 4-15=$5,000, 16-34=$19,750, 35-57=$46,450, 58-81=$69,675, 82+=$77,400
Gross Receipts; or Minimum Performance Requirement ($100/week starting year 3), whichever is greater

brand_name: Hand and Stone Massage and Facial Spa

royalty_basis: Transaction-based fee (% of property sale price, tiered by annual volume): 3% (1–4 properties/yr), 2% (5–9), 1.5% (10–14), 1.25% (15–19), 1% (20–24), 0.8% (25+ properties/yr). Paid per transaction. NOT a percentage of gross sales revenue.

Gross Revenues (combined licensing fee — no separate royalty/ad fund split in the Non-Traditional LDD). Payable monthly by the 20th of the following month. Covers each partial or full month of the License Agreement term.

Gross Revenue. Range 4%–5%: new franchisees building or purchasing from KFCLLC pay 5% (5/15 Amendment); Legacy Franchisees building new Outlets pay 4% (Legacy New Development Addendum). Minimum $1,440/month (CPI-adjusted). All royalty payments by electronic funds transfer.
Net Revenues (actual gross revenues collected from customers, cash or credit, plus all other revenues, excluding taxes collected from customers and refunds/adjustments)

No royalty fee. Matco Tools is a distributorship model — distributors purchase products from Matco at distributor pricing and resell from mobile truck/van at retail prices. Revenue to Matco comes from mandatory product purchases, not a percentage of distributor sales.
Gross Sales (total invoiced sales including labor, materials, services; excludes separately stated sales tax)
Gross Sales (all revenue less sales taxes)

Gross Sales. Standard rate 7.5%; may reduce to 6.75% if GS exceeds prior year by 110%, 6.5% at 120%, 6.0% at 130% (Partners in Profit incentive). Volume threshold reductions: GSMTS >$1.25M: 50% reduction on excess; GSMTS >$1.55M: 0% on excess. VetFran: 3.75% for first 52 weeks. Minimum $300/week.
Gross Receipts (Mitigation Services); separate flat rate for Reconstruction Services
Gross Room Revenues. May receive 0.5% rebate for meeting brand standards.
monthly Gross Volume (excluding Reduced Rate Services)
Net Sales (all revenue excluding sales tax)
Gross Revenues. Beginning on first full calendar month; minimum monthly Royalty Fee of $900.
Gross Revenues (total dollar income from all sales to customers)
Net Sales (gross sales of all services performed and invoiced, excluding sales taxes)
Gross Sales for the preceding week
